In an approximation used in flows driven by this force, inertial forces are assumed to be negligible in comparison to gravity. This force can be calculated from the surface integral of the Cauchy stress tensor in a fluid. The magnitude of this force is equal to the weight of fluid that would otherwise occupy its volume, according to Archimedes’ principle.
